annotations.label: Axiom of extensionality

anchor.snippet: Two sets are equal (are the same set) if they have the same elements.

annotations.note: ZFSet.ext is the `@[ext]` lemma stating two ZFC sets are equal iff they have the same elements.

annotations.label: Axiom of regularity

anchor.section: Axiom of regularity (also called the axiom of foundation) - 2
anchor.snippet: Every non-empty set

annotations.note: ZFSet.regularity states every nonempty ZFSet has an element disjoint from it.

annotations.label: No set is an element of itself

anchor.section: Axiom of regularity (also called the axiom of foundation) - 2
anchor.snippet: With the axioms of pairing and union, this implies that no set is an element of itself.

annotations.note: ZFSet.mem_irrefl proves x ∉ x for any ZFSet x.

annotations.label: Every set has an ordinal rank

anchor.section: Axiom of regularity (also called the axiom of foundation) - 2
anchor.snippet: this implies that every set has an ordinal rank

annotations.note: ZFSet.rank assigns an Ordinal to every ZFSet, with rank_lt_of_mem witnessing its monotonicity.

annotations.label: Axiom schema of specification

anchor.section: Axiom schema of specification (or of separation, or of restricted comprehension) - 3
anchor.snippet: The axiom schema of specification states that this subset always exists

annotations.note: ZFSet.sep with mem_sep gives `{y ∈ x | p y}` for any predicate, realizing separation.

annotations.label: Even integers as a subset

anchor.section: Axiom schema of specification (or of separation, or of restricted comprehension) - 3
anchor.snippet: the even integers can be constructed as the subset of the integers

annotations.note: This illustrative ZFC example of separating even integers from integers has no Mathlib counterpart at the ZFSet level.

annotations.label: Existence of the empty set

anchor.section: Axiom schema of specification (or of separation, or of restricted comprehension) - 3
anchor.snippet: the axiom schema of specification can be used to prove the existence of the empty set

annotations.note: ZFSet.empty defines ∅ : ZFSet with notMem_empty witnessing emptiness.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of the empty set

anchor.section: Axiom schema of specification (or of separation, or of restricted comprehension) - 3
anchor.snippet: The axiom of extensionality implies the empty set is unique

annotations.note: ZFSet.eq_empty characterizes ∅ by `x = ∅ ↔ ∀ y, y ∉ x`, implying uniqueness via ext, though no standalone uniqueness theorem is stated.

annotations.label: Axiom of pairing

anchor.snippet: there exists a set which contains

annotations.note: Pairing is realized by the Insert instance on ZFSet, with ZFSet.mem_pair characterizing membership in `{y, z}`.

annotations.label: Axiom of union

anchor.snippet: The axiom of union states that for any set of sets

annotations.note: ZFSet.sUnion (⋃₀) with mem_sUnion realizes the axiom of union.

annotations.label: Axiom schema of replacement

anchor.snippet: The axiom schema of replacement asserts that the image of a set under any definable function will also fall inside a set.

annotations.note: ZFSet.image with the Definable₁ typeclass takes the image of a definable function, realizing replacement.

annotations.label: Axiom of infinity

anchor.snippet: Then there exists a set X such that the empty set

annotations.note: ZFSet.omega together with omega_zero and omega_succ exhibits a set containing ∅ and closed under successor.

annotations.label: Subset

anchor.snippet: By definition, a set

annotations.note: ZFSet.Subset / HasSubset instance with subset_def gives the standard `∀ z ∈ x, z ∈ y` characterization.

annotations.label: Axiom of power set

anchor.snippet: The Axiom of power set states that for any set

annotations.note: ZFSet.powerset with mem_powerset realizes the power-set axiom.

annotations.label: Axiom of well-ordering (choice)

anchor.snippet: there exists a binary relation

annotations.note: exists_wellFoundedLT (Zermelo's well-ordering theorem) is proven on arbitrary types but not stated internally on ZFSet.

annotations.label: Choice function formulation

anchor.snippet: Let [MATH] be a set whose members are all nonempty.

annotations.note: ZFSet.choice with choice_isFunc and choice_mem provides a choice function on any ZFSet of nonempty sets.

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma equivalent to choice

anchor.snippet: A third version of the axiom, also equivalent, is Zorn's lemma

annotations.note: Zorn's lemma is formalized in Mathlib at the level of arbitrary types (zorn_le, zorn_partialOrder, etc.), equivalent to choice in Lean's foundations.

annotations.label: Choice trivial for finite sets

anchor.snippet: Since the existence of a choice function when

annotations.note: No ZFSet-level statement that choice on finite sets is provable from the other axioms is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characterization of V

anchor.snippet: It is provable that a set is in V if and only if the set is pure and well-founded.

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state a 'pure and well-founded ↔ in V' characterization (no IsPure predicate on ZFSet).

annotations.label: Montague non-finite axiomatizability

anchor.snippet: if ZFC is consistent, it is impossible to axiomatize ZFC using only finitely many axioms.

annotations.note: Montague's non-finite-axiomatizability of ZFC is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NBG equivalent to ZFC for set theorems

anchor.snippet: NBG and ZFC are equivalent set theories in the sense that any theorem not mentioning classes and provable in one theory can be proved in the other.

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal NBG theory or conservativity result of NBG over ZFC.

annotations.label: Gödel's second incompleteness theorem

anchor.snippet: says that a recursively axiomatizable system that can interpret Robinson arithmetic can prove its own consistency only if it is inconsistent.

annotations.note: Gödel's second incompleteness theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Consistency of ZFC unprovable in ZFC

anchor.snippet: Hence the consistency of ZFC cannot be proved within ZFC itself

annotations.note: Mathlib has no meta-theoretic statement about the unprovability of Con(ZFC) inside ZFC.

annotations.label: Consistency from inaccessible cardinal

anchor.snippet: The consistency of ZFC does follow from the existence of a weakly inaccessible cardinal

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Cardinal.IsInaccessible but does not derive Con(ZFC) from inaccessible cardinals.

annotations.label: Abian–LaMacchia independence results

anchor.snippet: they proved this subtheory consistent, and proved that each of the axioms of extensionality, replacement, and power set is independent of the four remaining axioms of this subtheory.

annotations.note: Abian–LaMacchia independence of extensionality, replacement, and power set is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Independence of regularity

anchor.snippet: Because there are non-well-founded models that satisfy each axiom of ZFC except the axiom of regularity, that axiom is independent of the other ZFC axioms.

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize non-well-founded set models or the independence of regularity.

annotations.label: Tarski's axiom implies infinity, power set, choice

anchor.snippet: Assuming that axiom turns the axioms of infinity

annotations.note: Tarski's axiom and its consequences for ZFC axioms are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Independence results via forcing

anchor.snippet: Forcing proves that the following statements are independent of ZFC

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of Cohen's forcing or independence results derived from it.

annotations.label: Diamond principle implications

anchor.snippet: The diamond principle implies the continuum hypothesis and the negation of the Suslin hypothesis.

annotations.note: The diamond principle, CH, and the Suslin hypothesis are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Martin's axiom implies Suslin hypothesis

anchor.snippet: Martin's axiom plus the negation of the continuum hypothesis implies the Suslin hypothesis.

annotations.note: Martin's axiom and the Suslin hypothesis are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constructible universe satisfies GCH etc.

anchor.snippet: The constructible universe satisfies the generalized continuum hypothesis

annotations.note: Mathlib has no constructible universe L or proof that L satisfies GCH/AC.

annotations.label: Kurepa hypothesis equiconsistency

anchor.snippet: The failure of the Kurepa hypothesis is equiconsistent with the existence of a strongly inaccessible cardinal

annotations.note: The Kurepa hypothesis and its equiconsistency with inaccessibles are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Independence of axiom of choice from ZF

anchor.snippet: A variation on the method of forcing can also be used to demonstrate the consistency and unprovability of the axiom of choice

annotations.note: Cohen's independence-of-choice result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Con(ZF) implies Con(ZFC)

anchor.snippet: Thus every model of ZF contains a submodel of ZFC, so that Con(ZF) implies Con(ZFC).

annotations.note: Gödel's L-based proof that Con(ZF) → Con(ZFC) is not in Mathlib.
